patch.value:
## Context

CONFIGURATION.md lines 59-63 document id_prefix, author_default, output.default_format, and locks.ttl_seconds as user-facing settings.
None appear in CONFIG_KEY_VALUES (src/cli/commands/config.ts:44-92) nor in NESTED_SETTING_DESCRIPTORS (src/core/config/nested-settings.ts).
The established pattern for adding leaf settings is to add a NestedSettingDescriptor entry — adding them requires no other code changes per the module's JSDoc.
Nested-settings path for each:
  id_prefix -> 'id_prefix'
  author_default -> 'author_default'
  output.default_format -> 'output.default_format'
  locks.ttl_seconds -> 'locks.ttl_seconds'

## Acceptance criteria

- pm config set id_prefix <prefix> updates id_prefix in settings.json
- pm config set author_default <name> updates author_default
- pm config set output_default_format <format> updates output.default_format
- pm config set locks_ttl_seconds <n> updates locks.ttl_seconds (integer, min 1)
- pm config get/list shows the new keys
- CONFIGURATION.md updated with example config set commands
- Shell completions updated

## Evidence

- docs/CONFIGURATION.md:59-63 (mentions but no config set examples for these keys)
- src/cli/commands/config.ts:44-92 (CONFIG_KEY_VALUES — none of the 4 keys present)
- src/core/config/nested-settings.ts (NESTED_SETTING_DESCRIPTORS — none of the 4 keys present)
- src/core/config/nested-settings.ts:25-48 (NestedSettingDescriptor pattern for new additions)
Expose id_prefix, author_default, output.default_format, locks.ttl_seconds via pm config set

id_prefix, author_default, output.default_format, and locks.ttl_seconds are listed in CONFIGURATION.md as configurable settings but have no pm config set key. They require manual editing of .agents/pm/settings.json. Adding them to NESTED_SETTING_DESCRIPTORS (the approved pattern for leaf settings) would make them script- and agent-friendly.

- pm config set id_prefix <prefix> updates id_prefix in settings.json
- pm config set author_default <name> updates author_default
- pm config set output_default_format <format> updates output.default_format
- pm config set locks_ttl_seconds <n> updates locks.ttl_seconds (integer, min 1)
- pm config get/list shows the new keys
- CONFIGURATION.md updated with example config set commands
- Shell completions updated

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Implemented via 4 NestedSettingDescriptor entries in src/core/config/nested-settings.ts (config.ts unchanged - it iterates the table dynamically at L433 and L1164). Entries: {key:id_prefix, path:id_prefix, kind:string, non_empty:true}; {key:author_default, path:author_default, kind:string, non_empty:true}; {key:output_default_format, path:output.default_format, kind:string, choices:[toon,json]}; {key:locks_ttl_seconds, path:locks.ttl_seconds, kind:integer, min:1}. SURPRISE: body guessed output.default_format choices were json/human; actual is toon/json per PmSettings types.ts:648 and validator vLiteral(toon,json) settings-validator.ts:390. Paths honored by validator: id_prefix=vString L386, author_default=vString L387, locks.ttl_seconds=vNumber int L389, output.default_format=vLiteral L390. Tests: extended tests/unit/commands/config-key-aliases-search.spec.ts (new general-setting aliases describe: path map, kebab/snake resolve, set/get round-trip, accept+reject for each). completion.ts needs NO entry (nested keys not statically listed). typecheck clean; config specs 106 green.

patch.value: Added: pm config set now supports id_prefix, author_default, output_default_format, locks_ttl_seconds via NESTED_SETTING_DESCRIPTORS (script/agent friendly, no hand-editing settings.json).
